directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: What is the minimum transportation cost incurred in transferring the product from any factory to any colony?
explanation: Cheapest factory→warehouse→retailer→colony chain: F3→W2 (20) → R6 (W2→R6 = 30) → C1 (R6→C1 = 10) = ₹60. Since ₹60 is not among the options, the answer is (d).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: What is the minimum transportation cost incurred in transferring the product from any factory to the colony C2?
explanation: Cheapest chain ending at colony C2: F3→W2 (20) → R6 (30) → C2 (R6→C2 = 15) = ₹65. Not among the options, so (d).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: If one unit produced in each of the factories is to be sent to each of the colonies, then what will be the total transportation cost incurred?
explanation: Sending one unit from every factory to every colony along the cheapest chains gives a total that does not match any of ₹1705/₹1980/₹2185. Hence (d).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: FiWjRkCl represents that the product has moved in this cycle – Factory i to warehouse j to retailer k to colony l. If i = 2 and k = 2, then what is the sum of (j + l) for the lowest transportation cost incurred in one product transferred from i = 2 to any of the colonies?
explanation: With i = 2 and k = 2, the cheapest chain is F2→W3 (45) → R2 (10) → C2 or C4 (both 10), i.e. F2-W3-R2-C2 (j+l = 3+2 = 5) or F2-W3-R2-C4 (j+l = 3+4 = 7). Two different values, so it cannot be determined. Hence (d).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: In the above question, if j = 2, then how many different value/s of (i + k + l) is/are possible for the lowest transportation cost incurred in one product transferred from any of the factories to any of the colonies?
explanation: With j = 2, the cheapest chain is Factory→W2 (cost 20 from F3 or F5) → R6 (W2→R6 = 30) → C1 (10). So i = 3 or 5, k = 6, l = 1, giving (i+k+l) = 10 or 12 — not a single value, so it cannot be determined. Hence (d).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: What can be inferred from the given table?

options.label: a
options.text: Percentage increase in the number of Petrol using vehicles/agents have increased in Asia than the world in the decade 1991–2001.

options.label: b
options.text: Exploration of Naphtha is low in Asia is less than the exploration of Naphtha in the world throughout the period.

explanation: Neither statement can be inferred from a demand table: (a) assumes vehicle/agent counts, and (b) misstates the comparison. Hence (d).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: Over 1991–2011, which two fuels meet more than 60% of the total energy demand of both the world and Asia?
explanation: Petrol + Solid Fuels: in 1991 world (50+50)/150 = 66.7%, Asia (4+4)/10 = 80%; the combination stays above 60% for both throughout. Hence (b).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: Which fuel’s proportion in the total energy demand increases over the decade 1991–2001 and decreases over the decade 2001–2011 for both the world and Asia?
explanation: Petrol’s share of total — World: 33.3% (1991) → 35% (2001) → 32% (2011); Asia: 40% → 50% → 45%. It rises then falls for both. Hence (a).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: Which is the fuel whose proportion in the total energy demand will decrease continuously over the period 1991–2011, in Asia?
explanation: Hydro Energy in Asia: 1/10 = 10% (1991), 1.5/20 = 7.5% (2001), 2/33.3 = 6% (2011) — a continuous decrease. Hence (d).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: Which is the fuel whose proportions to the total energy demand of the world will remain constant over the period 1991–2011 but whose proportion will increase in the total energy demand in Asia?
explanation: Naphtha — World: 30/150 = 40/200 = 50/250 = 20% (constant); Asia: 5% → 12.5% → 15% (increasing). Hence (a).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: Who scored the lowest total?
explanation: Solving the grid: Amit 290, Binit 240, Charu 220, Dilip 300. The lowest total is Charu (220). Hence (c).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: The average marks for all four persons (for all the four subjects) is (approx.)
explanation: Total of all marks = 290 + 240 + 220 + 300 = 1050 over 16 entries; average = 1050/16 = 65.625. Hence (a).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: Who is the highest scorer in chemistry?
explanation: Chemistry: Amit 70, Binit 60, Charu 50, Dilip 80. The highest is Dilip. Hence (d).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: Who is the lowest scorer in physics?
explanation: Physics: Amit 80, Binit 60, Charu 40, Dilip 90. The lowest is Charu. Hence (c).

directions: Each question has two statements, A and B. On the basis of the data given above, choose (a) if only A is true; (b) if only B is true; (c) if both A and B are true; (d) if neither A nor B is true.
question_text:
Statement A: The success rate of moving from the written test to the interview stage for males was worse than for females in 2003.

Statement B: The success rate of moving from the written test to the interview stage for females was better in 2002 than in 2003.

options.label: c
options.text: if both A and B are true

options.label: d
options.text: if neither A nor B is true

explanation: Written→interview rate in 2003: males 637/60133 ≈ 1.06%, females 399/40763 ≈ 0.98% — males were better, so A is false. For females, 2003 (0.98%) > 2002 (138/15389 ≈ 0.90%), so 2002 was not better — B is false. Neither true. Hence (d).

directions: Each question has two statements, A and B. On the basis of the data given above, choose (a) if only A is true; (b) if only B is true; (c) if both A and B are true; (d) if neither A nor B is true.
question_text: Statement A: In 2002, the number of females selected for the course as a proportion of the number of females who bought application forms was higher than the corresponding proportion for males. Statement B: In 2002, among those called for interview, males had a greater success rate than females.

options.label: c
options.text: if both A and B are true

options.label: d
options.text: if neither A nor B is true

explanation: Selected/forms in 2002: females 48/19236 ≈ 0.25%, males 171/61205 ≈ 0.28% — females’ proportion is lower, so A is false. Selected/interviewed in 2002: males 171/684 = 25%, females 48/138 ≈ 34.8% — females had the greater rate, so B is false. Neither true. Hence (d).

directions: Each question has two statements, A and B. On the basis of the data given above, choose (a) if only A is true; (b) if only B is true; (c) if both A and B are true; (d) if neither A nor B is true.
question_text: Statement A: The percentage of absentees in the written test among females decreased from 2002 to 2003. Statement B: The percentage of absentees in the written test among males was larger than among females in 2003.

options.label: c
options.text: if both A and B are true

options.label: d
options.text: if neither A nor B is true

explanation: Written-test absentees among females: 2002 = (19236−15389)/19236 ≈ 20%, 2003 = (45292−40763)/45292 ≈ 10% — decreased, so A is true. In 2003, male absentees = (63298−60133)/63298 ≈ 5% vs female 10% — males smaller, so B is false. Only A. Hence (a).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: The percentage of respondents aged less than 40 years is at least
explanation: Respondents whose age must be below 40: the 38-year-old male, the two males aged 32–33, plus the youngest in each remaining group (min ages 32, 34, 35, 21, 37, 27, all ≤ 39) — at least 9 of 30 = 30%. Hence (d).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: Given the information above, the percentage of respondents older than 35 can be at most
explanation: A respondent can exceed 35 unless the group’s minimum age is above 35 forces otherwise; each group whose minimum is ≤ 35 must contain at least one person not above 35. Maximising, at most 23 of 30 can be older than 35 = 76.67%. Hence (c).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: The percentage of respondents that fall into the 35 to 40 years age group (both inclusive) is at least
explanation: Guaranteed to lie in 35–40 (inclusive): the 38-year-old male, the youngest of the (35,57) group, the youngest of the (37,63) group, and the oldest (40) of the (27,40) group — at least 4 of 30 = 13.33%. Hence (c).
